ops.title: SocketServer: v3 sessions, serial accept queue, bind/shutdown order

ops.body:
# Serial accept queue (the GCD trap — unchanged since v1)

The accept loop runs on a **serial** DispatchQueue (`acceptQueue`, label-only construction = serial), never a concurrent one: a blocking `accept(2)` on a concurrent queue makes GCD spawn a fresh worker thread per pending block, exploding the thread pool. The serial queue holds exactly one blocked accept. Each accepted descriptor is then served by an unstructured Swift Task (not a queue); only blocking codec READS run on a per-session serial readQueue.

# v3 session discipline (v2: 7623022; v3: bb42d99)

Persistent multiplexed exact-v3 sessions over the SessionTransport.swift codec (length-prefixed frames, fail-closed SessionTransportError — the twin of wire/frame.go). v3 split the stream machinery into SocketChunkStream.swift (bounded chunk streams) and SocketResponse.swift; wire/stream.go is the Go counterpart. The lifeproto ENVELOPE version is independent of the transport and remains {"v":2}. `trust.check(descriptor:)` (PeerTrust) runs BEFORE the handshake and any frame processing; a failing peer's descriptor closes with no session and no reply. The handshake is bounded only per-read: SO_RCVTIMEO is set to `handshakeTimeout` best-effort (the setsockopt result is ignored) and limits each blocking read, NOT total handshake wall time — a peer making intermittent progress can extend the handshake indefinitely. Empty builds are refused on both sides. Request streams are bounded by `streamQueueDepth`; **SocketServer.start() and SocketClient.init reject nonpositive depths outright** (SocketBoundedChannel's internal max(1, capacity) clamp is unreachable through the public paths). Server events push through the session's serialized writer (`SocketSession.pushEvent` under the codec write lock).

# Bind and shutdown

start(): an existing path gets a bare connect(2) probe. Probe SUCCESS refuses the bind (addressInUse). **Any probe FAILURE unconditionally unlinks the path and rebinds** — the probe does not prove "dead socket": a regular file at the path, or a live socket whose connect failed transiently, is removed too. chmod 0600 runs before listen; its result is ignored. stop(): stops intake, disconnects sessions, joins their request tasks (`acceptQueue.sync {}` joins the accept loop), then unlinks the path. Safe to call more than once.

# Historical (v1, replaced in 7623022)

One request line in, one reply line out per connection, \n-framed and capped at maxLineBytes; per-connection SO_RCVTIMEO bounded socket READS but never the handler, so shutdown could block behind a stuck handler. Replaced wholesale by the framed session protocol above.
